candidates.name: tokenization
candidates.blurb: How raw text becomes the integer IDs models actually see — BPE, WordPiece, SentencePiece, and why tokenizer quirks explain so many LLM failures.
candidates.note: rich material for a link-first page (Karpathy's build, HF course chapter, Tiktokenizer playground); no homegrown interactive needed

sources.label: Neural Machine Translation of Rare Words with Subword Units (Sennrich et al., 2015)
Let's Build the GPT Tokenizer (Andrej Karpathy, 2024)
Tokenizers chapter, Hugging Face LLM Course (Hugging Face, 2022)
Tiktokenizer (dqbd, 2023)

candidates.name: transformer block
candidates.blurb: The repeating unit of every transformer: attention + feed-forward, wrapped in residual connections and normalization. The piece the existing primitives compose into.
candidates.note: two excellent external interactives (Transformer Explainer, LLM Visualization) — the page links these instead of this site building one

sources.label: Attention Is All You Need (Vaswani et al., 2017)
The Illustrated Transformer (Jay Alammar, 2018)
The Annotated Transformer (Harvard NLP, 2022)
Transformer Explainer (Polo Club, Georgia Tech, 2024)
LLM Visualization (Brendan Bycroft, 2023)

candidates.name: MAE / masked image modeling
candidates.blurb: BERT's fill-in-the-blank idea applied to pixels: hide most of an image's patches and train a ViT to reconstruct them (MAE, BEiT).
candidates.note: papers are clear but no canonical deep-dive or interactive exists — the page carries most of the explanation itself

sources.label: Masked Autoencoders Are Scalable Vision Learners (He et al., 2021)
BEiT: BERT Pre-Training of Image Transformers (Bao et al., 2021)
Masked Autoencoders Are Scalable Vision Learners — Paper Explained (AI Coffee Break with Letitia, 2021)

candidates.name: VQ-VAE
candidates.blurb: An autoencoder with a discrete codebook bottleneck — images become grids of tokens, which is what lets transformers generate them (VQ-VAE, VQGAN, DALL-E 1).
candidates.note: one excellent intuition-first blog plus the papers; no interactive exists — the page leans on Snell's deep-dive

sources.label: Neural Discrete Representation Learning (van den Oord et al., 2017)
Understanding VQ-VAE — DALL-E Explained Pt. 1 (Charlie Snell, ML@Berkeley, 2021)
Taming Transformers for High-Resolution Image Synthesis (Esser, Rombach and Ommer, 2020)

candidates.name: rectified flow
candidates.blurb: Straighten the noise-to-data paths a flow model learns so sampling takes a handful of steps instead of hundreds — the variant of flow matching behind SD3.
candidates.note: author's own intro plus papers; no third-party explainer — the page (or a section of flow-matching) does the explaining

sources.label: Flow Straight and Fast: Learning to Generate and Transfer Data with Rectified Flow (Liu, Gong and Liu, 2022)
Rectified Flow: An Introduction (Qiang Liu, UT Austin, 2022)
Scaling Rectified Flow Transformers for High-Resolution Image Synthesis (Esser et al., Stability AI, 2024)

candidates.name: Perceiver
candidates.blurb: Cross-attend a huge input into a small fixed-size latent array, then run the transformer there — one architecture for images, audio, point clouds, anything.
candidates.note: papers plus a thorough HF walkthrough; no visual explainer — low urgency given the architecture's niche status

sources.label: Perceiver: General Perception with Iterative Attention (Jaegle et al., 2021)
Perceiver IO: A General Architecture for Structured Inputs and Outputs (Jaegle et al., 2021)
Perceiver IO: a scalable, fully-attentional model that works on any modality (Hugging Face, 2021)

candidates.name: retrieval-augmented architectures
candidates.blurb: Bolt a search step onto a generator: fetch relevant documents at inference time and condition on them (RAG, RETRO) instead of memorizing everything in weights.
candidates.note: strong companions ready for a future page (RAG + RETRO papers, Alammar's illustrated RETRO)

sources.label: Retrieval-Augmented Generation for Knowledge-Intensive NLP Tasks (Lewis et al., 2020)
Improving Language Models by Retrieving from Trillions of Tokens (Borgeaud et al., 2021)
The Illustrated Retrieval Transformer (Jay Alammar, 2022)
